title: Table: The tables below show the distribution of students at UK universities by mode of...
description: The tables below show the distribution of students at UK universities by mode of study and gender. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT NHANH

IELTS Writing Task 1 – Postgraduate Students (1996–2010)

1. Hiểu đề (1 câu)

Bảng cho biết số lượng học viên sau đại học nam và nữ theo 2 hình thức học (full-time, part-time) qua 4 giai đoạn.

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

→ The tables compare the number of male and female postgraduate students studying full-time and part-time between 1996 and 2010.

3. Overview (chỉ 2 ý)

  • Full-time: tăng mạnh ở cả nam và nữ
  • Part-time: luôn cao hơn full-time, nhưng biến động nhẹ (tăng rồi giảm)

4. Body chia theo mode (dễ viết hơn chia theo gender)

BODY 1 – Full-time
  • Nam: tăng đều (75k → 87k → 115k → 151k)
  • Nữ: tăng tương tự (62k → 85k → 118k → 146k)

Key: cả hai giới đều tăng mạnh, gần như song song

BODY 2 – Part-time
  • Nam: giảm nhẹ rồi tăng, sau đó giảm (113k → 108k → 138k → 117k)
  • Nữ: tăng mạnh rồi giảm nhẹ (108k → 125k → 172k → 163k)

Key: luôn cao hơn full-time, nữ cao hơn nam ở cuối kỳ

5. So sánh nhanh (chèn vào body nếu muốn ăn điểm)

  • Ban đầu: nam \> nữ (full-time)
  • Cuối kỳ: số liệu gần bằng nhau (full-time)
  • Part-time: nữ vượt nam rõ ở 2005–2010

The tables compare the numbers of male and female postgraduate students studying full-time and part-time between 1996/97 and 2009/10.

Overall, full-time enrolment increased steadily for both genders, while part-time figures fluctuated. Men initially outnumbered women in part-time study, but women became the dominant group by the end of the period.

For full-time study, both genders showed consistent growth. The number of male students rose from 75,370 in 1996/97 to 151,275 in 2009/10, while the female figure increased from 62,344 to 146,980. Although men remained slightly higher overall, the gap narrowed significantly over time.

In contrast, part-time study showed more variation. Male participation fell slightly from 113,961 to 108,770 in 2000/01, then rose to a peak of 138,505 before declining to 117,105 in 2009/10. Meanwhile, the number of female part-time students increased steadily from 108,702 to 172,640 in 2005/06, before dropping slightly to 163,340. By the final year, women clearly outnumbered men in part-time education.
